response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, which demonstrates the performance-engineering mindset this role needs. However, his professional C++ experience is limited to a short stint in 2014–2015, he lists no Rust, and his recent years have centered on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than routing pipelines or navigation systems. The domain overlap and systems background make him worth a conversation, but the core language requirement is a significant gap.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map contributor
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source tooling, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Professional C++ experience is dated (2014–2015) and brief; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, routing tiles, or navigation guidance systems
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, away from systems-level pipeline engineering
